title: Open Buro Applications Manifest
description: An Application manifest is the array of applications a consumer fetches in order to discover and resolve capabilities.

application.description: An application that provides one or more capabilities.

id.description: Unique, stable, technical identifier for the application, in reverse-DNS notation (lowercase, dot-separated, at least two labels).

name.description: Human-readable display name, e.g. shown in a chooser when several applications match. Can be used by screen readers. Default when no localization is available

localizedName.description: Localized display names keyed by BCP 47 language tag.

url.description: Application base URL. Used for CSP and message origin verification.

version.description: Version of this manifest entry's format, used by the consumer to know how to parse the entry. This is not the application's own version number. A single manifest may mix applications of different format versions.

icon.description: Optional URL to the application's icon, shown in the chooser when several applications match.

capabilities.description: The actions this application can perform.
capabilities.$comment: If we end up with two lists (consumers and providers), we might have consumer applications with no capabilities.

application.$comment: Capabilities must be defined, even if empty.

capability.description: A single action an application can perform.

action.description: `PICK`: the consumer asks the application for one file, many files, or a folder. `SAVE`: the consumer sends one file, many files, or a folder to the application.

path.description: Endpoint that fulfils this capability. Absolute URL. Should return a UI for the user to pick or save files, and then return the picked or saved file(s) to the consumer.

iframeAllow.description:
Permissions Policy features the application needs when opened in an iframe. The consumer uses this list to build the iframe `allow` attribute. When omitted, the application needs no extra features. Ignored for non-iframe targets.
See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/iframe#allow

mimeTypes.description: MIME filters the capability accepts (e.g. any file, or only images for a gallery).

multiple.description: Whether the capability can pick or save multiple files.

permissionsPolicyFeature.description:
A Permissions Policy directive name, as used in the iframe `allow` attribute (lowercase, hyphen-separated).
See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Feature-Policy#directives

mimePattern.description: A full MIME type, a subtype wildcard (`image/*`), or the catch-all `*/*`.
